User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CollabData platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

Dataset.title: Dataset
Dataset.description: Represents a dataset uploaded by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the dataset entity.

uploadDate.description: Date and time when the dataset was uploaded.

fileType.description: Type of the dataset file (e.g., CSV, XLSX, JSON).

storagePath.description: Path to where the dataset is stored.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project for collaborative data analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the project entity.

creationDate.description: Date and time when the project was created.

userIDs.description: References to Users. (Relationship: User N:N Project)

Analysis.title: Analysis
Analysis.description: Represents a specific analysis performed on a dataset.

id.description: Unique identifier for the analysis entity.

analysisType.description: Type of analysis performed (e.g., Descriptive Statistics, Histogram).

parameters.description: Parameters used for the analysis (e.g., column selections, filters).

resultsPath.description: Path to where the analysis results (e.g., plots, tables) are stored.

executionDate.description: Date and time when the analysis was executed.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. This collection uses path-based ownership, ensuring only the authenticated user can access their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores projects owned by a specific user. Uses path-based ownership for security.  Projects contain a 'userIDs' array that holds the list of users with access to the project.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project.

definition.description: Stores datasets owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'projectId' to associate datasets with projects, as well as denormalized userIDs from the project for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who uploaded the dataset.

params.name: datasetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the dataset.

definition.description: Stores analyses related to a specific project. Includes denormalized userIDs from the project for authorization independence.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project the analysis belongs to.

params.name: analysisId
params.description: The unique identifier of the analysis.

definition.description: Stores shared project metadata. Includes an array of 'userIDs' indicating project membership for collaborative access.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support a collaborative data analysis platform, CollabData, with features like user authentication, dataset uploads, shared projects, and dynamic EDA. The design emphasizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability.

Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization.  Specifically, project membership information (`userIDs` in the `Project` entity) is copied into the `Dataset` and `Analysis` documents, enabling rules to validate access to these sub-collections without `get()` calls. Project ownership is enforced on project creation, and membership is managed through the `userIDs` array.

Structural Segregation is applied by storing user-specific data (projects and datasets) under user-specific paths (`/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}` and `/users/{userId}/datasets/{datasetId}`), while shared project data is segregated under `/projects/{projectId}` and contains denormalized access control information.

Access Modeling leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative data.  The data model uses a hierarchical path structure for entities that represent a User 1:N Entity relationship (e.g. User has many Projects) as well as continuing the hierarchy for nested data.

This structure supports QAPs (Query Authorization and Privacy) by enabling list operations that are secure by design.  Because access control data is denormalized into each document, rules can efficiently validate `list` operations based on the `request.auth.uid` and document data without relying on expensive `get()` operations.
